So OPEC decides to flex on America and suddenly everyone's fighting over gas like it's the last iPhone on Black Friday. Three hundred percent price increases because apparently oil sheiks have bills to pay too.

The government's brilliant solution to an oil crisis is making everyone drive like they're delivering wedding cakes. Fifty-five mph speed limits because nothing says 'energy conservation' like turning every highway into a parking lot.

Detroit executives wake up one day and realize that maybe, just maybe, cars that get six miles per gallon aren't the future. The muscle car dies not with a roar but with a pathetic fuel-efficient whimper.
Oil prices go up and suddenly everything costs more because apparently every single thing in America is secretly made of petroleum. Your toothbrush? Oil. Your dreams? Also oil.
Mall developers look at their car-dependent shopping temples and think 'maybe people won't drive thirty miles for Orange Julius anymore.' Revolutionary thinking from the retail industry.
Congress throws money at solar panels like they just discovered the sun exists. Eighteen months later we're still burning fossil fuels but hey, at least some lab techs got new equipment.

And here's what really gets me: after all this geopolitical chaos, economic upheaval, and societal transformation, some poor pizza delivery guy can't afford his usual slice because his moped broke down and the only place he can walk to charges EIGHTEEN DOLLARS for a burrito. EIGHTEEN DOLLARS! For beans and rice wrapped in a tortilla! This is the real crime against humanity!
